features.title: Temporal retriever (recency / time-window weighting)
acceptance: Temporal retriever behind the EXISTING Retriever interface (kind 'temporal' added to RETRIEVER_KINDS); ranks by recency (exponential decay) with an optional time-window; owns a persistent SQLite index (index/remove) mirroring the keyword retriever

Passes the shared retriever conformance suite; fuses with the other signals (weighted RRF, per-candidate attribution); wired into the Local profile hybrid search and exposed on the Runtime for ingestion to populate
Deterministic + offline tests (injected clock); workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: the 5th retrieval signal (FR-24), fully additive. `'temporal'` added to RETRIEVER_KINDS (single source of truth) → apps/api retrieverKindSchema (z.enum, DERIVED) picks it up automatically; the compiler treats RetrieverKind as an opaque tag (no switch) and the web SignalBadge already mapped `temporal` (--chart-5) — so no consumer changed. createTemporalRetriever mirrors the keyword retriever's owned-index pattern: it takes a SQLite `db`, owns retrieval_temporal(ref PK, ts), index/remove, and retrieve() returns most-recent refs (ORDER BY ts DESC, ref ASC, optional ts>=now-window) scored by exponential recency decay 2^(-age/halfLife) in (0,1] (best-first, monotonic → satisfies conformance ordering); clock injected for determinism; timestamps normalized from number(ms)|ISO|Date, invalid → ValidationError. Wired into createLocalRuntime's hybrid set + exposed as Runtime.temporal (like keyword) for ingestion to populate. +8 tests (conformance + newest-first + decay(0.5 at one half-life) + window filter + limit/idempotent-reindex/remove + invalid-ts + a fusion test where a recent item outranks an older one and carries both temporal+keyword attribution). Effects E-012 (widened; adapter+conformance) + E-014 (profile wiring + Runtime). Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (retrieval 31) · build 15. Deferred: per-QUERY time windows (would change the shared RetrievalQuery contract → API ripple; construction-time window only here), config-exposed fusion weights (F-020), ingestion populating the index (same corpus seam as keyword/semantic).

features.title: Compiler compression stage (citation-preserving)
acceptance: The compress stage compresses over-budget fragments (deterministic, offline, query-relevant extractive summarization) instead of dropping them, preserving each fragment's citation (ref + provenance) and never exceeding the budget
Compression is surfaced: the fragment's whyIncluded notes original→compressed tokens and the compress trace stage summarizes it; LLM-based/pluggable compression stays a documented seam (F-020)
Unit + integration tests (compression salvages an otherwise-dropped fragment, keeps query-relevant content, preserves ref/provenance, never exceeds budget); workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: the compress stage now COMPRESSES over-budget fragments instead of only dropping them (FR-31), fully inside @tessera/context-compiler (no domain/API schema change). New compressToFit (stages/compress-text.ts): deterministic, offline, EXTRACTIVE summarization — segments text into lines→sentences (conservative .!? split leaves code intact), scores each by query-term overlap, greedily keeps the most-relevant segments that fit the token target, restores original order, never exceeds target, returns undefined if not even one segment fits. compress.ts: fitToBudget→compressToBudget(items, budget, {query}) — whole fragments pass through; an overflowing one is compressed to the remaining budget (if ≥ MIN 16 tokens) preserving its citation (same ref/provenance), else dropped-and-continue (graceful degradation preserved); never exceeds budget. Surfacing (no new fields): assemble uses the excerpt + appends 'compressed to fit budget (N→M tokens)' to whyIncluded; compiler adds a compress trace note (count + tokens saved). LLM/abstractive + pluggable compressor = F-020 (documented seam). +8 tests (compress-text: relevance/order/never-exceed/undefined/deterministic; compress: whole-passthrough/compress-salvage/graceful-skip/never-exceed; compiler integration: over-budget top fragment salvaged as a cited excerpt, package fits budget, trace note). Effect E-013 (compress behavior; no ContextPackage/API shape change → E-003 untouched). Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (context-compiler 25) · build 15.

features.title: Compiler reproducibility + caching + pluggable stage strategies
acceptance: Pluggable stage strategies (FR-34): the compressor and ranker are injectable strategies (default = current behavior), swapped WITHOUT any REST/MCP API or ContextPackage schema change

Reproducible & cacheable (FR-33): a deterministic compilation key over the normalized request + config fingerprint; an injectable CompilationCache (in-memory LRU adapter) short-circuits identical compiles to the same package (same inputs → same key/package)
Contained to @tessera/context-compiler; unit + integration tests (key determinism, cache hit/miss, strategy swap changes output + key, reproducibility); workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: reproducibility + caching + pluggable stage strategies (FR-33/34), fully inside @tessera/context-compiler as NEW OPTIONAL compiler options — no ContextPackage/REST/MCP shape change (FR-34 'without API change'). strategies.ts: CompressionStrategy (default extractiveCompression wrapping F-019's compressToFit) + RankStrategy (default defaultRankStrategy wrapping rankCandidates); each carries an id folded into the key. cache.ts: CompilationCache port + createInMemoryCompilationCache (LRU, get/set bump recency, evict past maxEntries). key.ts: computeCompilationKey(normalizedRequest, fingerprint) = sha256 of canonical JSON of {task, budget, EFFECTIVE retrievalLimit, sorted filter kinds} + fingerprint {rankStrategy id, compression id, dedupThreshold?, expandDepth?} — deterministic, order-independent for kinds; the fingerprint MUST include every output-affecting knob or the cache goes stale. compiler.ts: options gain compression?/rankStrategy?/cache?; pipeline extracted to runPipeline(); when a cache is present, compile() keys the normalized request, returns a hit VERBATIM (identical package → true reproducibility) else compiles once and stores; rank via rankStrategy.rank, compress via compression.compress. compressToBudget gained an optional compress fn (default compressToFit). +9 tests (key determinism/sensitivity/order-independence; LRU get/set/evict; integration: cache hit skips retrieval + identical package + miss on different request, no-cache reproducibility, compressor swap changes the excerpt, ranker swap changes order — all with unchanged package shape). Effect E-013 (compiler options + key; no API shape change → E-003 untouched). Deferred: exposing the key on ContextPackage/API, fine-grained per-stage incremental recompute, persistent/shared cache backend, LLM compressor (the strategy seam enables it). Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (context-compiler 34) · build 15. (Also flipped F-019 status backlog-slip fixed here.)

features.title: Realtime updates (SSE/WebSocket)

Events flow through an injected ApiEventBus (composition seam); the memory-capture route is a real producer (emits memory.captured); the OpenAPI document lists the endpoint

features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: realtime updates via Server-Sent Events (FR-38) — ADDITIVE to the REST surface, no change to existing routes/schemas or MCP. events.ts: typed ApiEventBus over the core EventBus (ApiEventMap = document.ingested/document.removed/memory.captured; JSON-safe, non-sensitive summaries) + sseFrame/sseComment. routes/v1/events.ts: GET /v1/events reply.hijack()s and owns reply.raw — writes text/event-stream with a retry hint + ': connected' handshake, SUBSCRIBES BEFORE the handshake (no lost event between connect and subscribe), heartbeats on an unref'd timer, and tears down subscriptions+timer on request 'close'. buildServer gains an events? option (default createApiEventBus()); the memory-capture route is a REAL producer (emits memory.captured with lineageId/kind/title). SSE-transport (x-accel-buffering:no, cache-control:no-transform) so proxies don't buffer. +4 unit (framing + bus emit/unsub) + 4 e2e over a REAL socket (listen:0 + fetch stream reader): handshake, an emitted bus event delivered, POST /v1/memory → memory.captured streamed, and /v1/events documented in OpenAPI. features.title: Postgres + pgvector adapters + Docker Compose stack
acceptance: Postgres RelationalStore adapter (pg + drizzle node-postgres) and a pgvector VectorStore adapter, each implementing the existing storage ports and passing the SHARED conformance suites
A Docker Compose stack (pgvector/pgvector image) stands up Postgres+pgvector for self-hosted; the adapters are verified against it

Postgres conformance is env-guarded (skipped without a reachable DB, like the transformers/ollama pattern) so default gates stay green; verified green against the real container; ADR records the choice
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R1 must; unblocked once Docker Desktop was installed): Postgres + pgvector storage adapters under the EXISTING ports — no port/consumer change (ADR-0026). createPostgresStore (pg + drizzle-orm/node-postgres; Drizzle db handle, migrate/healthcheck/close) mirrors the SQLite RelationalStore. createPgVectorStore implements VectorStore over pgvector via RAW SQL (vector(N) column; <-> L2 / <=> cosine; vectors as $n::vector text literals — NO pgvector npm dep, only pg added; lazy CREATE EXTENSION+table; identifier-validated table name; ON CONFLICT upsert; dimension validation). Both pass the SAME shared conformance suites (port parity with SQLite). docker-compose.yml = pgvector/pgvector:pg16 service (healthcheck+volume) — the self-hosted stack. features.title: Multi-tenancy + org RBAC
acceptance: AuthProvider port in @tessera/api with a local/none adapter (a single default tenant + a full-access principal → today's zero-auth local behavior is unchanged) and an OIDC + RBAC cloud adapter as a documented, env-guarded seam; a principal is a user or a scoped, revocable API token (NFR-2)

Tenancy + RBAC domain model: an org/workspace tenant scope + roles (owner/admin/member/viewer) mapped to a permission catalog (memory:read/write, search/compile/effects:read, admin:manage); the /v1 boundary authenticates every request into an AuthContext {principal, tenantId, roles} and authorizes each route against its required permission, returning the standard {error} envelope (401 UNAUTHORIZED / 403 FORBIDDEN)

Scoped, revocable API tokens via a TokenStore port + local adapter (hashed at rest, scopes, revoke); org/workspace isolation is carried on AuthContext.tenantId and threaded to the request, with cross-tenant data-plane row-scoping (domain stores) + live OIDC verification kept as documented, env-guarded seams (mirroring the F-023/F-015 local-first + cloud-seam pattern)

Additive & back-compatible: existing routes + e2e stay green under the default local (no-auth) profile; an ADR records the auth/tenancy approach and the still-open OIDC-library question; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (first R2 feature): the API auth control plane (FR-52/FR-54/NFR-2) — tenancy + RBAC + scoped tokens, fully in @tessera/api under new ports (ADR-0028), ADDITIVE and back-compatible. auth/model.ts: TenantId + DEFAULT_TENANT_ID, ROLES (owner/admin/member/viewer), a PERMISSIONS catalog + ROLE_PERMISSIONS, Principal/AuthContext, and effectivePermissions = roles' permissions ∩ a token's scopes (least privilege). AuthProvider port (auth/provider.ts): createLocalAuthProvider = NONE (full-access default principal in the default tenant → today's zero-auth local behavior, unchanged) + createTokenAuthProvider (requires a valid Bearer; missing/invalid → 401). TokenStore port (auth/token-store.ts) + createInMemoryTokenStore: crypto-random 'tsk_' secrets HASHED AT REST (SHA-256; plaintext returned once), verify/revoke/list. Enforcement (auth/plugin.ts): registerAuth adds an onRequest hook resolving request.authContext via the injected provider (skips public routes) + requirePermission(p) per-route guards; buildServer gains auth? (default local none); /v1/openapi.json marked public. Per-route guards: search→search:read, compile→compile:read, effects→effects:read, memory GET→memory:read, memory POST/PATCH→memory:write. Errors reuse the envelope (UnauthorizedError→401, ForbiddenError→403; @tessera/core already had both codes). Because the default is the none provider, every existing route + e2e stays green (unauthenticated → full access). +21 unit (model 8 · token-store 6 · provider 7) + 7 auth e2e (401/403/200/revoked/public-openapi + zero-auth back-compat). Effects E-003 (additive 401/403 paths; response schemas + OpenAPI unchanged) + new E-018 (auth/tenancy/RBAC contract). Gates green workspace-wide: format · typecheck 28 · lint 16 · test 28 (api 36) · build 16 · e2e 15 (api 25). DELIBERATELY DEFERRED as documented, env-guarded seams (precedent F-023/F-021): live OIDC verification (OIDC is just another AuthProvider; the concrete IdP/library stays an open ADR), data-plane per-tenant row isolation (AuthContext.tenantId is resolved+carried, but the domain stores are not yet tenant-scoped — no cross-tenant guarantee beyond the boundary is claimed), composition-root wiring (config/server env → choose provider; a one-line seam, capability proven by the token-provider e2e), a persistent SQLite TokenStore, advertising the Bearer scheme in OpenAPI, and MCP gateway auth+quotas (F-026).

features.title: MCP gateway (multi-client auth + quotas)
acceptance: The MCP surface brokers clients with per-call authentication + RBAC authorization, reusing the F-025 auth model (AuthProvider/AuthContext/Permission) via a TYPE-ONLY import from @tessera/api (no Fastify in the MCP runtime); each tool declares a required permission (search→search:read, compile_context/explain→compile:read, get_effects→effects:read, capture_memory→memory:write) and is denied with a clean masked envelope (UNAUTHORIZED / FORBIDDEN) when the client's principal lacks it

Per-principal quotas: a QuotaLimiter port + in-memory fixed-window adapter (injected clock) meters tool calls per principal; exceeding the limit surfaces a RATE_LIMITED error (new @tessera/core error code → 429 at the REST envelope), metered independently per principal

Additive & back-compatible: buildMcpServer takes an optional gateway (auth + quota + credential resolver); with no gateway the five tools behave exactly as before (existing e2e green). Verified by unit tests (authorize + quota guard) + a real-MCP-client e2e over InMemoryTransport (a restricted principal is denied a tool; quota exhaustion returns RATE_LIMITED); an ADR records the design; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R2): MCP gateway — multi-client auth + quotas (FR-36), additive to @tessera/mcp (ADR-0029). REUSES the F-025 auth model TYPE-ONLY (AuthProvider/AuthContext/AuthInput/Permission imported as types from @tessera/api) so the MCP runtime stays Fastify-free (F-012 invariant preserved — the gateway needs only ctx.permissions.has()). gateway.ts: createMcpGateway({auth, quota?, resolveCredential?}) → guard(tool, extra) = authenticate (→AuthContext, UnauthorizedError on bad/missing) → authorize the tool's required permission (TOOL_PERMISSIONS: search→search:read, compile_context/explain→compile:read, get_effects→effects:read, capture_memory→memory:write; ForbiddenError) → meter the principal (RateLimitedError). Errors flow through the EXISTING masked envelope (toEnvelope) — 4xx-safe codes surfaced. quota.ts: QuotaLimiter port + createInMemoryQuotaLimiter (fixed-window, injected clock, independent per-principal buckets). buildMcpServer(services, {gateway?}) wraps each tool with the guard; DEFAULT off = the five tools behave exactly as before. resolveCredential(extra) reads the SDK authInfo/Authorization header (default) — transport-agnostic; stdio carries one identity, the multi-client HTTP transport is a seam. New shared @tessera/core RATE_LIMITED code + RateLimitedError → 429 at the REST envelope (statusForCode/codeForStatus/errorCodeSchema + web mirror updated; additive, compiler-guided by the exhaustive switch); MCP surfaces it as a code. +18 tests: mcp unit 14 (quota 4 · gateway 8 · explain 2) + mcp e2e 11 (gateway 4 over a REAL MCP client/InMemoryTransport: viewer denied capture_memory=FORBIDDEN + reads allowed, member allowed, quota exhaustion=RATE_LIMITED, default-no-gateway back-compat) + api error-handler RATE_LIMITED→429. Effects E-018 (MCP consumer of the auth model + quota port) + E-006 (core RATE_LIMITED) + E-003 (guarded tools; 429 envelope; SDK regen = no diff since the error envelope isn't in the OpenAPI). Gates green workspace-wide: format · typecheck 28 · lint 16 · test 28 (mcp 14, api 36) · build 16 · e2e 15 (mcp 11). DELIBERATELY DEFERRED as documented seams: the multi-client HTTP/streamable transport + auth middleware (populates authInfo), a persistent/distributed quota store, token-bucket/sliding-window + per-tool weights + quota headers, and composition-root wiring (config/server → construct the gateway from a profile; capability proven by the e2e injecting the real F-025 token provider).

features.title: Governance & audit UI + full audit trail
features.notes: Done 2026-07-04 (R3's first feature): governance & audit UI + full audit trail (FR-48/FR-55/NFR-13; ADR-0034), delivered in three verifiable increments. (1a) Audit-trail backend in @tessera/api (apps/api/src/audit): a Fastify-free AuditEvent model + AuditLog port (record/query/prune/forTenant, append-only, tenant-scoped via ADR-0033) + createInMemoryAuditLog (newest-first, filter action/actor/outcome/time, seq-cursor pagination, retention prune); events are NON-SENSITIVE (ids/actions/outcomes only, NFR-7). recordAudit = an onResponse hook recording routes flagged config.audit (search/compile/effects.read/memory.read|write/audit.read); actor+tenant from AuthContext, outcome from status (>=400→denied); failure-isolated. GET /v1/audit (admin:manage, tenant-scoped, filter+paginate) → OpenAPI + regenerated @tessera/sdk (getAudit); buildServer gains audit? (default in-memory). (1b) Persistent createSqliteAuditLog in @tessera/config (audit_events table, seq rowid, tenant column, prune) — type-only @tessera/api import keeps config Fastify-free (ADR-0030, verified); config.audit section (enabled, retention {maxAgeDays,maxEntries}) + TESSERA_AUDIT_* env (.env.example) + Runtime.audit + apps/server wiring. (2) Web UI (@tessera/web): /audit (AuditView — filter by action/outcome, provenance-first events table, empty/error/loading states) + /governance (GovernanceView — RBAC roles→permissions matrix + retention posture) consuming GET /v1/audit via lib/api (getAudit/useAudit); a 'Govern' nav group in BOTH nav sources (app-shared sidebar + lib/nav palette); RBAC/action catalogs mirrored in lib/governance.ts (ADR-0022). Enterprise-grade + WCAG AA (axe e2e clean; outcome badges use accessible colored-text after axe caught a solid-destructive contrast fail); screenshot-verified. Effects E-020 (new — audit-trail contract) + E-003 (GET /v1/audit route/schema/SDK) + E-014 (config audit wiring) + E-018 (consumes the auth model: actor/tenant + admin:manage). Gates green workspace-wide: state (37 features, 20 effect-links) · format · typecheck 30 · lint 17 · test 30 (api 49 incl. audit conformance 6; config 22 incl. sqlite-audit persist) · build 17 (+/audit +/governance) · e2e 16 (api 34 incl. success/denied+admin-only+cross-tenant isolation; web 7 incl. 2 new axe). DEFERRED as documented seams: MCP-surface audit recording (same AuditLog injectable), full user management/SSO (FR-48 'users' beyond roles display), data export/delete (DSR) + configurable encryption (NFR-13), a distributed/external audit sink, and periodic multi-tenant retention scheduling (prune method + policy exist).
acceptance: Audit trail backend: an AuditLog port + in-memory (reference) and SQLite adapters recording append-only AuditEvents (id, tenantId, actor {principalId, kind}, action, target, outcome success|denied, timestamp, non-sensitive metadata) for sensitive actions (FR-55: access/reads of governed surfaces, memory/config writes, admin, billing, exports); tenant-scoped via forTenant (ADR-0033); passes a shared conformance suite incl. cross-tenant isolation; never logs secrets/raw content (NFR-7)

The API records audit events for sensitive actions via an injected AuditRecorder (default local sink) and exposes GET /v1/audit (admin:manage, tenant-scoped, filterable by action/actor/outcome + time window, paginated) returning the trail; Zod-validated → OpenAPI + regenerated SDK; a configurable retention policy (max age/count) prunes the trail (NFR-13); wired through @tessera/config (Runtime.audit) + the server

Governance & audit UI (@tessera/web, FR-48): an Audit Log view (filter by action/actor/outcome/date, provenance-first) + a Governance view (users/roles from the RBAC catalog, retention settings) consuming /v1/audit via the typed client; enterprise-grade per DESIGN-SYSTEM.md, WCAG AA, screenshot-verified; nav entry added

Additive & back-compatible (audit defaults to a local sink; existing routes/tools/e2e stay green); an ADR records the audit-trail + governance design; workspace gates green incl. e2e (a cross-tenant audit e2e + web a11y)

features.title: Billing & subscriptions (cloud) behind a Billing port (Dodo Payments)
acceptance: A @tessera/billing package (open-core): a BillingProvider port + domain (Plan + a PLANS catalog free/pro/enterprise carrying entitlements; Subscription with status + current-period) + a local/free adapter (the OSS default — zero external deps, every tenant on the free plan) + an in-memory SubscriptionStore; a Dodo Payments adapter (fetch-based, no SDK per ADR-0024) implementing the same port, env-guarded, with offline-unit-tested HMAC webhook-signature verification (live Dodo API calls remain a documented seam)

A REST billing surface in @tessera/api: GET /v1/billing/plans (public), GET /v1/billing/subscription + POST /v1/billing/checkout (guarded by admin:manage, tenant from the AuthContext), POST /v1/billing/webhook (public, signature-verified) — Zod-validated, in the OpenAPI doc, consistent error envelope

Dashboard billing UI + live Dodo verification are documented seams
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R2): billing & subscriptions behind a Billing port, open-core (OQ4 resolved 2026-07-03; ADR-0011 confirmed + ADR-0031). NEW @tessera/billing package (deps: @tessera/core only — no Fastify, so config/MCP stay clean): domain.ts (PlanId free/pro/enterprise + PLANS catalog carrying Entitlements; Subscription + SubscriptionStatus; effectiveEntitlements falls back to free for un-entitled statuses); ports.ts (BillingProvider + SubscriptionStore + CheckoutRequest/Session + BillingEvent); adapters/local.ts (createLocalBilling — the OSS default: every tenant active-free, checkout/webhook rejected, zero external deps); adapters/dodo.ts (createDodoBilling — fetch-based, no SDK per ADR-0024; verifyDodoSignature = constant-time HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body; parseDodoEvent maps Dodo webhook → BillingEvent; env-guarded, live API = seam); subscription-store.ts (in-memory; persistent = seam). CONFIG (@tessera/config): a `billing` section (provider none|dodo, dodoBaseUrl) + TESSERA_BILLING_* env; createRuntimeBilling reads Dodo secrets via the SecretsProvider; Runtime.billing + ApiServices.billing selected by config (default local/free). REST (@tessera/api): /v1/billing/plans (public), /v1/billing/subscription + /v1/billing/checkout (admin:manage, tenant from AuthContext), /v1/billing/webhook (public, signature-verified via an ENCAPSULATED raw-body string parser so other routes keep normal JSON) — Zod schemas → OpenAPI; falls back to createLocalBilling when no provider is wired. Reused the existing admin:manage permission (no catalog ripple). +18 tests: billing 13 (domain 4 · local 2 · dodo 7) + api billing e2e 5 (plans public, free subscription, checkout-rejected-locally, a SIGNED Dodo webhook over HTTP updates the subscription, bad-signature→401). SDK regenerated (openapi.json + generated schema.ts now include /v1/billing). Effects E-003 (new routes) + E-014 (config wiring) + NEW E-019 (billing contract). Gates green workspace-wide: format · typecheck 30 · lint 17 · test 30 (billing 13, api 36) · build 17 · e2e 16 (api 30). DEFERRED as documented seams: live Dodo API verification (no account/keys here) — reconcile verifyDodoSignature with Dodo's Standard Webhooks format when wiring live; a persistent SubscriptionStore; entitlement ENFORCEMENT (wiring PLANS limits into the compiler budget / MCP quotas); and the dashboard billing UI (R3-adjacent). This completes R2's buildable features (F-025/F-026/F-030 + the F-034 wiring seam).

A persistent SQLite TokenStore (createSqliteTokenStore over the storage Drizzle handle) implements the @tessera/api TokenStore port (hashed at rest, revoke, list) so issued tokens survive restarts; createLocalRuntime exposes Runtime.auth = { provider, tokenStore? } selected by config.auth.mode

The runnable server wires it end-to-end: startApiServer passes the AuthProvider to buildServer; startMcpServer builds the MCP gateway (auth + optional in-memory quota) and passes it to startMcpStdio (which gains a gateway passthrough); a `tessera-token` bin issues a token (secret printed once) so token mode is usable

features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R2 follow-up seam #1): made F-025/F-026 auth usable end-to-end — config-driven AuthProvider + MCP gateway selection + a PERSISTENT token store (ADR-0030), additive (default `none` = unchanged). KEY CONSTRAINT SOLVED: apps/server boots the MCP process THROUGH @tessera/config, which imports @tessera/api type-only to stay Fastify-free; constructing auth providers pulls @tessera/api runtime (→ Fastify). Fix: a NEW Fastify-free subpath export `@tessera/api/auth` (apps/api/src/auth/core.ts = model + providers + token store, EXCLUDES the Fastify plugin.ts; package.json exports['./auth']). config builds runtime.auth from that subpath → the MCP runtime stays Fastify-free (verified: config's only runtime @tessera/api import is /auth; the auth-core graph has zero fastify). WHAT: (1) config schema `auth` section (mode none|token, tenant, quota {enabled,limit,windowMs}) + TESSERA_AUTH_* env mapping + merge (bool helper). (2) createSqliteTokenStore(db) — persistent TokenStore over the storage Drizzle handle (api_tokens table; hashed at rest via the shared hashApiTokenSecret/newApiTokenSecret helpers extracted from the in-memory adapter; revoke/list; survives restarts). (3) Runtime.auth {provider, tokenStore?} selected by config.auth.mode in createLocalRuntime. (4) @tessera/mcp startMcpStdio(services, {gateway?}) passthrough. (5) apps/server: startApiServer passes runtime.auth.provider to buildServer; startMcpServer builds createMcpGateway(auth + optional createInMemoryQuotaLimiter) → startMcpStdio; new `tessera-token` bin issues a scoped token (secret printed once) so token mode is usable. features.title: OIDC AuthProvider adapter (hosted login) + auth.mode=oidc
acceptance: An IdP-agnostic OIDC AuthProvider adapter (createOidcAuthProvider) in @tessera/api/auth: verifies a Bearer JWT against the IdP's JWKS (jose; remote JWKS by default, an injectable key set for tests), validates issuer/audience/expiry, and maps claims → Principal (sub→id, a roles claim → RBAC roles filtered to the catalog, a tenant claim → AuthContext.tenantId); missing/invalid/expired → UnauthorizedError (401). Fastify-free (jose only) so config/MCP stay clean

Config auth.mode gains `oidc` with an `oidc` section (issuer, audience, jwksUri, rolesClaim, tenantClaim) + TESSERA_AUTH_OIDC_* env (documented in .env.example, enforced by the env-docs guard); createRuntimeAuth builds the provider via the @tessera/api/auth subpath

Additive & back-compatible (default none unchanged); offline tests (generate an RSA keypair, sign JWTs, verify: valid→context with mapped roles/tenant, expired→401, wrong aud/iss→401, missing→401, role-claim mapping); ADR records the jose choice + IdP-agnostic standard-OIDC design; workspace gates green. A live IdP end-to-end check is a documented seam
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R2): IdP-agnostic OIDC AuthProvider (NFR-2; ADR-0032) — the 'which IdP/library' open question dissolved under the port model (Tessera VERIFIES tokens, it doesn't run an IdP/login). apps/api/src/auth/oidc.ts: createOidcAuthProvider verifies a Bearer JWT against the IdP's JWKS with `jose` (jwtVerify + createRemoteJWKSet; injectable keySet for offline tests), checks iss/aud/expiry, maps claims → Principal (sub→id; a roles claim (array | space/comma string) filtered to the RBAC catalog, unknown/empty → defaultRole viewer; a tenant claim → AuthContext.tenantId; name/email → displayName); any failure → UnauthorizedError (401, generic message). Exported from the Fastify-free @tessera/api/auth core so config/MCP stay clean (jose ≠ Fastify — verified: auth-core dist has no fastify). Config: auth.mode gains `oidc` + an auth.oidc section (issuer/audience required via superRefine, jwksUri/rolesClaim/tenantClaim optional) + TESSERA_AUTH_OIDC_* env (documented in .env.example — the new env-docs guard would have caught it); createRuntimeAuth builds it via the subpath. New dep: jose@^6 on @tessera/api. +10 tests: api oidc 7 (offline — generate an RSA keypair, sign JWTs: valid→mapped context, space-delimited roles + default tenant, unknown-role→viewer, missing/expired/wrong-aud/wrong-iss→401) + config 3 (auth/billing defaults, oidc accepted with issuer+audience, oidc rejected without them). Effects E-018 (OIDC adapter realized) + E-014 (config oidc wiring). Gates green: format · typecheck 30 · lint 17 · test 30 (api 43, config 19) · build 17 · e2e 16 (regression-clean). DEFERRED as documented seams: a LIVE IdP round-trip (no IdP available here), opaque-token introspection, and an OIDC discovery-document auto-config / login helper.

features.title: Data-plane per-tenant row isolation (FR-52 org/workspace isolation)
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R2 COMPLETE — the last R2 must): real per-tenant row isolation (FR-52) across every domain store, closing the F-025 seam. ADR-0033. Design: promote the tenant primitive TenantId + DEFAULT_TENANT_ID to @tessera/core (dependency-free base; @tessera/api/auth/model re-exports — public API unchanged, E-006), then add a `forTenant(tenantId): Self` scoped view to every store/retriever/service whose base is bound to DEFAULT_TENANT_ID (so every existing test + the zero-auth Local profile are byte-for-byte unchanged; tenancy engages only when a non-default tenantId is threaded). Enforcement lives IN the adapter (a tenant column / composite PK / vec0 per-tenant table injected on write + filtered on every read), not a bypassable wrapper. Per store: VectorStore.forTenant (sqlite-vec = per-tenant vec0 table; pgvector = tenant column + composite PK (tenant,id); shared vector conformance gained an isolation case — pgvector VERIFIED LIVE against the Docker container). MemoryStore/MemoryService.forTenant (memories.tenant_id + additive ALTER migration; isolation conformance). GraphStore/KnowledgeGraphService.forTenant (nodes/edges tenant_id + composite PK since nodeIdFor(kind,key) is deterministic; tenant predicate in every query AND both arms of the getEffects recursive CTE; isolation conformance). Retriever.forTenant on the port + all 5 retrievers + HybridRetriever.forTenant (keyword FTS5 tenant column, temporal composite PK — both filter their owned indices; semantic/graph/symbolic delegate to the scoped stores; keyword/temporal have adapter isolation tests). ContextCompiler.forTenant (scopes retriever + graph; folds a non-default tenantId into the CompilationKey so a shared cache stays tenant-safe). NO wire change: Memory/ContextPackage/CompileRequest/REST/MCP schemas keep NO tenant field — isolation is a server-side storage guarantee driven by AuthContext.tenantId → OpenAPI + generated SDK unaffected. Threading: REST routes call services.X.forTenant(tenantOf(request)) (new auth helper; default → 'default'); MCP tool handlers use guard()'s returned AuthContext.tenantId (ungated → default). Effect-link ripple handled: @tessera/observability instrumentServices' tracing Proxy special-cases forTenant (re-wrap the scoped service, don't Promise-ify it) — E-015. +tests: storage vector isolation (sqlite-vec + pgvector-live) · memory + graph store isolation conformance (in-memory + sqlite) · keyword + temporal adapter isolation · compiler forTenant isolation + key.tenant · api auth e2e cross-tenant memory (acme writes, globex 404s + empty list). Gates green workspace-wide: state (37 features, 19 effect-links) · format · typecheck 30 · lint 17 · test 30 (storage 37 w/ PG, memory 27, knowledge-graph 25, retrieval 33, context-compiler 36) · build 17 · e2e 16 (api 33 incl. cross-tenant, mcp 11, web 5 WCAG). Effects E-018 (data-plane isolation REALIZED — no longer a seam) + E-001/E-007/E-010/E-011/E-012/E-013 (each port gained forTenant + isolation conformance) + E-006 (core TenantId) + E-003 (routes/tools thread tenantId; schemas unchanged) + E-015 (observability Proxy). DEFERRED as documented seams: Postgres-backed memory/graph stores (still SQLite-only; they'll inherit the same tenant_id contract), cross-tenant admin ops, per-tenant quotas/usage + tenant lifecycle (create/delete/migrate), and ingestion populating per-tenant retrieval/vector indices (same forTenant seam).

features.title: Live corpus indexing: ingested documents populate fragments + keyword/semantic/temporal indices
acceptance: A persistent indexing DocumentSink (composition root) writes ingested documents as blob fragments (existing FragmentSource contract) AND indexes them: keyword (FTS5), temporal (timestamps), semantic (embed → VectorStore, batched + content-hash-cached so unchanged chunks are never re-embedded, NFR-12); document removal removes index entries; re-scan is idempotent (no duplicate index rows)

search/compile answer from the user's actual repository: an integration test ingests a fixture repo through the real pipeline and asserts POST /v1/search returns its files with multi-signal attribution and POST /v1/compile produces a budget-bounded package citing them

Captured memories are indexed for retrieval too: memory capture/edit (API + MCP) upserts into the keyword/semantic/temporal indices (supersede updates the index entry) — the 2026-07-04 live check proved a just-captured memory is unfindable via /v1/search today, so this path is part of closing the loop, not only ingestion

Tenant scoping respected end-to-end (indices written through the forTenant/scoped views); chunking strategy documented (plan); embedding remains offline-testable (fake provider drives the deterministic tests, transformers env-guarded)
Workspace gates green; compile-cache invalidation on index writes addressed (at minimum: document the staleness contract; ideally key on a corpus version)
features.notes: Done 2026-07-04 (R3 must): the core loop is CLOSED — F-038 makes scans run + land documents in the corpus; F-039 makes that output RETRIEVABLE, so search/compile answer from the user's actual repository. @tessera/config createCorpusIndexer = one tenant-aware path that lands (ref, text) in the blob corpus AND all three indices — keyword (FTS5, FR-22), temporal (recency, FR-24), semantic (embed → VectorStore, FR-21) — through the forTenant scoped views (ADR-0033); an in-memory ref→sha256(text) cache never re-embeds an unchanged (ref, text) (NFR-12), belt-and-suspenders over the F-006 manifest. createIndexingDocumentSink (replaces the F-038 blob-only sink; skip binary; removal clears corpus + every index; timestamp from git commit / fs mtime) + createIndexingMemoryService (a MemoryService decorator: API/MCP captures AND auto-extracted memories index under memory/<lineageId>, an edit re-indexing the same ref) write through the SAME indexer → one shared ref space (document.id / memory/<lineageId>) so a search hit always resolves in the compiler (the fusion requirement). @tessera/retrieval: KeywordRetriever.remove(ref) added (additive) for document removal. Local profile wires the indexer; services.memory is the indexed service. FIX: memory refs are '/'-delimited — a ':' becomes an NTFS alternate-data-stream on Windows (broke blob list()); caught + fixed while wiring. Verified: an integration test scans a fixture repo then asserts search returns its files with MULTI-SIGNAL attribution (keyword+temporal+semantic) AND compile cites them budget-bounded; a just-captured memory is findable and an edit updates the index; the corpus-indexer unit proves the NFR-12 cache (counting fake embeddings) + remove clears all three signals + tenant isolation. Gates green workspace-wide: format · typecheck 30 · lint 17 · test 30 (config 33, retrieval +1 keyword-remove) · e2e 16 · build 17 · state valid (21 effect-links). DEFERRED/documented: sub-document CHUNKING (whole-document indexing here; the sink seam is unchanged for chunking + embedBatch later); compile-cache staleness (the Local runtime enables NO compilation cache, so index writes are immediately reflected — if enabled later it MUST key on a corpus version); multi-tenant ingestion-pipeline scoping (ingestion indexes in the default tenant; memory in its capture tenant); the F-061 search-depth UI. Effects E-008/E-012/E-013 (index writers realized) + E-014/E-021 (composition + runtime sink evolved) + E-010 (memory indexing decorator).

features.title: Code-symbol extraction → live knowledge-graph population + static effect-links + manual assertion surface (resolves OQ5)
acceptance: OQ5 decided by ADR before code (tree-sitter vs LSP vs TS compiler API; local-first, multi-language direction with TS/JS first-class); an ingestion processor extracts files/symbols/imports into GraphStore nodes/edges (file/symbol/module) incrementally (a re-scan updates only changed files' subgraphs)

Delivered in 5 verifiable increments. (1) @tessera/knowledge-graph: additive GraphStore.removeNode (node + incident edges) + removeEdges(filter) primitives (port + in-memory + sqlite + conformance + service) for incremental subgraph replacement. (2) @tessera/ingestion: a SymbolExtractor port (extract(document)→ExtractedGraph|undefined) + resolve-import (relative→extensionless source-relative keys, so './b' matches file b.ts; bare/out-of-repo skipped) + createGraphExtractionSink (a DocumentSink writing file/symbol nodes + defines/imports edges through a STRUCTURAL GraphWriteService — the real KnowledgeGraphService is assignable, no ingestion→knowledge-graph runtime dep, F-017 seam pattern; deriveStaticEffectLinks inverts imports; INCREMENTAL: each upsert replaces the file's outgoing edges + clears its stale incoming effect-links via removeEdges, without clobbering other files' edges). (3) @tessera/config: createTreeSitterSymbolExtractor over web-tree-sitter (WASM) + prebuilt tree-sitter-wasms grammars (TS/JS/TSX by extension; lazy Parser.init + per-language grammar/query cache; a tree-sitter Query captures imports + top-level decls; fully offline) — wired into the runtime sink tee. (5) records. Verified: a config integration test scans a fixture repo (a.ts imports ./b) through the REAL tree-sitter extractor and asserts get_effects({kind:'file', key:'b'}) returns 'a' with a path, idempotent on re-scan; the graph-extraction sink unit proves incremental correctness (removed import drops the stale effect-link; file removal preserves other files' edges) over the real KnowledgeGraphService; api/mcp e2e for the assertion path (assert → get_effects returns it; viewer 403). Effects E-002/E-011 (graph population + removeNode/removeEdges) + E-009/E-021 (ingestion writer) + E-003 (POST /v1/effects + assert_effect + SDK) + E-018/E-020 (effects:write + effects.write).

apps/web migrates from lib/api to the generated @tessera/sdk (ADR-0022's deferred completion) with an auth-aware fetch; all existing views keep working (search/inspector/audit/governance tests stay green)
e2e covers both modes (zero-auth + token mode against a real token-mode server); WCAG AA; workspace gates green

features.title: Account & profile: professional profile page, API-token self-service, admin user management
acceptance: A professional /profile page (DESIGN-SYSTEM-grade): identity (name/email/avatar initials), roles + effective permissions, tenant/org, plan (from billing), connected-agent hints; no fake data — every field backed by the API

API-token self-service: /v1/tokens routes (list/create with scopes + expiry/revoke; secret shown exactly once) over the existing TokenStore port, RBAC-guarded + audited, OpenAPI + SDK regenerated + MCP parity tool (ADR-0036); UI in profile/settings with copy-once UX

Admin user management view (FR-48 completion): principals/roles listing + role assignment where the store supports it (local token principals today; OIDC-user listing = documented seam); the hand-mirrored RBAC catalog in apps/web/lib/governance.ts is replaced by a shared/SDK-derived catalog (drift risk flagged in the 2026-07-04 dashboard review)
axe AA + screenshot-verified; e2e (issue token in UI → authenticate with it against the API); workspace gates green

features.title: Compliance completion: memory retention policies, DSR export/delete, MCP-surface audit
acceptance: Retention & expiry policies per memory kind/scope (FR-15 — flagged R2 but never delivered): config-driven, applied by a prune pass (schedule seam), superseded-version pruning honoring the never-silently-mutate contract; policy reads/writes audited

DSR (NFR-13): tenant-scoped data export (a complete JSON bundle: memories + lineages, graph, sources, audit trail) and tenant data delete, admin:manage-guarded, audited, e2e-verified against a seeded tenant; documented for operators
MCP-surface audit recording (closes the F-027 seam): gateway-guarded tool calls record AuditEvents through the same injected AuditLog (actor/tenant/tool/outcome), verified by an MCP e2e
Workspace gates green; encryption-at-rest posture documented (SQLite/Postgres options + key handling guidance) in docs

features.title: Full-stack E2E: real server + fixture repo + real web + real MCP client (new e2e-full gate)
acceptance: A new full-stack e2e suite mimics an actual user + environment: boot the REAL @tessera/server (Local profile, temp dirs, ephemeral port; fake embeddings by default, real transformers env-guarded) → register a fixture repository as a source → scan completes → indexed

Human journey: Playwright drives the REAL apps/web (pointed at that live API — not its own stub server): sign-in (token mode) → sources shows the scan → search returns repo content → inspector compiles a cited package → capture a memory → audit shows the trail

Agent journey: a real MCP client session against the same runtime exercises search/compile_context/get_effects/capture_memory (+ add_source per ADR-0036) and asserts token-lean, budget-bounded responses
Registered as the e2e-full gate (gates.json planned→active with this feature) + a CI job; deterministic fixture, flake-budgeted; workspace gates green

features.title: Performance benchmarks & budgets: NFR-4 enforced (latency + token-efficiency) + web-perf activation
acceptance: A repeatable benchmark harness over a versioned fixture corpus measures: search p95 (< 300 ms local), compile p95 (< 2 s at default budget), incremental ingest latency, and MCP/REST tokens-per-answer (ADR-0036 token-lean requirement) — results emitted as a machine-readable report + tracked in-repo

Registered as the perf gate (gates.json planned→active) with thresholds failing CI on regression; NFR-4 stops being an unbenchmarked claim (this was an R0 exit criterion left unmeasured — flagged 2026-07-04)
web-perf gate ACTIVATED for apps/web (bundle-size budget + Lighthouse CI): heavy views verified code-split, initial bundle lean (ADR-0021 finally realized)
Any misses produce tuning work items (not silently lowered thresholds); workspace gates green

features.title: Multi-project workspaces: (tenantId, projectId) scope across the data plane + project switcher (ADR-0037)
acceptance: Project entity + scope model per ADR-0037: adapter-enforced (tenantId, projectId) isolation extending forTenant across vector/memory/graph/retrieval/fragments/sources/compile-cache keys; reserved `default` project keeps every existing test + single-project deployment byte-for-byte unchanged; conformance suites gain project-isolation cases

Control plane: /v1/projects CRUD + MCP tools (ADR-0036 parity) + audited lifecycle; REST/MCP project selection (one documented mechanism in OpenAPI; omitted → default), project id validated against the caller's tenant on every request

Dashboard: project switcher in the app shell + create/manage projects; 'New project' lives INSIDE the project switcher (+ command palette), per the 2026-07-04 product decision — the sidebar's dedicated 'New memory' button is NOT replaced but evolves into a single '+ New' quick-create menu (memory / source / project; contextual default) so the frequent capture action stays one click away while project creation gets a first-class home; per-project sources/search/compile verified in e2e (cross-project isolation: project A's content never surfaces in project B)
Migration assigns existing rows to the default project via the F-024 runner (additive, reversible); workspace gates green incl. e2e

features.title: Marketing site (apps/marketing) on the apex domain — positioning, features, pricing, trust (ADR-0035)
acceptance: New apps/marketing (@tessera/marketing) Next.js app per ADR-0035 with its own service-scoped AGENTS.md; DESIGN-SYSTEM tokens; static-first (no authenticated API calls, holds no credentials — NFR-17); enterprise-grade visual quality (frontend bar: screenshot-verified)

Pages: home (hero, how-it-works, the three differentiators — Context Compiler, effect-links, governance — beyond the unabyss.com reference), features, pricing rendered from the @tessera/billing PLANS catalog (no hand-copied numbers), enterprise/trust (security/compliance posture), and a /skills route (placeholder until F-054); CTAs → the app subdomain
SEO baseline: per-page metadata, OG images, sitemap.xml, robots.txt + llms.txt (agent-readable index, ADR-0036); Core Web Vitals budget wired into the web-perf gate for this app
axe WCAG AA e2e; workspace gates green (new app joins typecheck/lint/test/build/e2e pipelines + CI)

features.title: @tessera/cli: one-command onboarding — init/serve/source add/token issue/mcp-config emit/doctor (ADR-0036)
acceptance: A new @tessera/cli package (bin `tessera`): `init` scaffolds config + data dir and boots the Local profile (API + MCP) with sensible defaults; `serve` runs it; `source add <path|git-url>` registers + scans via the F-038 surface; `token issue` wraps the F-034 flow; `doctor` checks node/config/storage/embeddings health

`mcp-config` emits ready-to-paste MCP client config for the major agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Codex CLI, Continue — table-driven so new agents are data, not code); the one-command story `npx @tessera/cli init` → a connected agent works end-to-end and is proven by an integration test
Production-grade CLI UX: clear errors, --json output mode for agents (ADR-0036), no interactive-only paths (every prompt has a flag), works on Windows/macOS/Linux (NFR-10)
Unit + integration tests (boots real Local runtime in temp dirs); npm publish pipeline lands with F-059; workspace gates green

features.title: Docs site (apps/docs, Fumadocs) on the docs subdomain: quickstart, concepts, API reference, agent guides (ADR-0035)
acceptance: New apps/docs (@tessera/docs) built with Fumadocs per ADR-0035 (MDX in-repo, versioned with the code, built-in search), DESIGN-SYSTEM tokens, service-scoped AGENTS.md; llms.txt + llms-full.txt served (ADR-0036)

Information architecture: Quickstart (the F-052 one-command path), Concepts (Context Compiler, effect-links, memory, knowledge graph, provenance — from the PRD/architecture, rewritten for users), How-to guides (sources, tokens, tenants/projects, governance/audit), Reference (generated API reference from the /v1 openapi.json via the SDK generate artifact + MCP tool reference + CLI reference + config/env reference from .env.example)

Per-agent MCP setup guides (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Codex, Continue) with copy-paste config; deployment guides section EXISTS with the Local + self-host (compose) pages (cloud deploy pages complete in F-056)
SEO + CWV budget (NFR-17, web-perf gate); axe WCAG AA e2e; a docs link-check in CI (no broken internal links); workspace gates green

features.title: Skills registry: first-party agent skills (SKILL.md) + /skills page + install via download/CLI/MCP (ADR-0036)
acceptance: A versioned skills/ registry in-repo: first-party SKILL.md skills that teach agents Tessera workflows — at minimum: compile-before-coding (use compile_context instead of reading whole files), effects-before-editing (get_effects before touching a symbol), capture-memory (record decisions/lessons after work), project-onboarding (bootstrap a new repo into Tessera); each skill has a manifest (name/description/version/compatible agents) validated in CI
The /skills page on the marketing site (beyond unabyss.com/skills: category filters, per-skill detail with what-it-does/why/install) renders FROM the registry (no hand-copied content)
Install paths: direct download, `tessera skills install <name>` (CLI), and an MCP list_skills/get_skill tool so a connected agent can fetch skills without a browser
Skills tested against a real agent session (documented transcript or scripted MCP e2e); axe AA on the page; workspace gates green

features.title: Remote MCP: HTTP/streamable transport with Bearer auth through the gateway (auth + quotas + audit)
acceptance: The MCP server is reachable over the SDK's streamable-HTTP transport (served by apps/server alongside REST, config-gated), with Bearer credentials populating authInfo so the EXISTING F-026 gateway authenticates, authorizes (RBAC), meters quotas, and (with F-047) audits every call — remote MCP never bypasses the gateway
stdio remains the local default; the HTTP transport requires a non-none auth mode (no unauthenticated remote MCP, NFR-2); session lifecycle + connection teardown are clean under client disconnect
e2e: a real MCP client connects over HTTP with a token issued by the F-034 store, calls tools with RBAC + quota enforcement verified; unauthenticated connection rejected
Docs (F-053) gain the remote-MCP setup page; OpenAPI/infra docs updated; workspace gates green

features.title: Self-hosted profile completion + deployment artifacts: PG memory/graph stores, S3, BullMQ, Dockerfiles, full compose, release CD, deploy guides
acceptance: Profile `self-hosted` boots for real: Postgres-backed MemoryStore + GraphStore adapters (same conformance suites incl. tenant/project isolation, env-guarded like F-023), S3-compatible BlobStore adapter, BullMQ/Redis Queue adapter — createRuntime selects them by config with NO domain change (FR-53; closes the F-023 deferral where non-local profiles throw)

Deployment artifacts: production Dockerfiles for apps/server (API+MCP) and apps/web (+ marketing/docs), a full docker-compose stack (server, web, postgres+pgvector, minio, redis) that stands up a working self-hosted Tessera from scratch, verified end-to-end
Release CD (NFR-15): tagged releases build + publish container images (and prep npm publish for sdk/cli); CI gains the compose-boot smoke

Deployment guides in docs (F-053 section completed): self-host via compose, managed-cloud walkthrough (a mainstream provider end-to-end incl. TLS, domains per ADR-0035 topology, env/secrets, backups via F-024), production checklist (auth mode, CORS allowlist, rate limits, retention, telemetry); workspace gates green

features.title: Analytics & usage: per-tenant metering, persistent subscriptions, analytics dashboard + billing UI
acceptance: Usage metering (NFR-12): a persistent per-tenant usage store (compiles/searches/ingested docs/tokens compiled, time-bucketed) fed at the API/MCP boundary; monthly-compile entitlement enforcement closes the F-035 seam; usage queryable via /v1/usage (admin-guarded, OpenAPI + SDK)
Persistent SubscriptionStore (closes the F-030 in-memory seam) so plan state survives restarts; webhook-driven updates verified

Dashboard: an Analytics view (FR-47 — retrieval quality proxies, usage, latency from the observability metrics, cost posture) + a Billing view (current plan, entitlements, usage vs. limits, Dodo checkout/upgrade flow behind the existing port); provenance-first charts (Recharts per DESIGN-SYSTEM)
axe AA + screenshots; e2e incl. metered request → usage visible; workspace gates green

features.title: Feature flags + plugin permissions & health (FR-57/59/60 completion)
acceptance: Feature flags (FR-57): a config-backed FlagProvider port (static local adapter; remote provider = seam) evaluated per tenant at the API boundary; flags surfaced read-only in the dashboard settings
Plugin health (FR-59 completion): health() in the plugin lifecycle + host aggregation exposed on /ready details; failure isolation extended with restart/backoff policy

Plugin permissions (FR-60): capability/permission declarations in the plugin manifest (least privilege — e.g. a connector declares network/filesystem needs) validated at load, denied-by-default beyond declarations, surfaced in the host API
Unit + integration tests; workspace gates green; ADR if the flag/permission model deviates from the plugin-host ADR-0020 envelope

features.title: Launch readiness: license (open-core), SBOM/supply-chain, release process, npm publish, repo polish
acceptance: License executed per OQ4 (open-core): LICENSE file(s) for the OSS core + the enterprise/cloud boundary documented (which packages are which), package.json license fields set, NOTICE.md verified complete; README rewritten for launch (current state, quickstart via CLI, links to docs/marketing) — the 2026-07-04 review found it still says 'pre-coding'

Supply-chain (NFR-18): SBOM generated in CI (CycloneDX), dependency pinning/audit policy enforced, provenance/signing for published artifacts (npm --provenance, image signing), SECURITY.md (vuln reporting policy)

Release process: versioning + changelog tooling (changesets or equivalent, ADR), tagged release workflow publishing npm packages (@tessera/sdk, @tessera/cli) + images (with F-056), release checklist in the harness governance docs
Brand/domain checklist from ADR-0008 executed (domain + trademark verified before public launch); state gate green (this feature is largely docs/CI — verification = state + build + CI run evidence)

features.title: Dashboard: live Overview — real workspace stats + SSE activity feed + notifications
acceptance: A new GET /v1/stats workspace summary (indexed documents, memories, graph nodes + effect-links, sources, last scan) — Zod → OpenAPI + regenerated SDK + a get_stats MCP tool (ADR-0036 parity), tenant-scoped; token-lean response

The Overview page consumes it: the stat cards show REAL values (the 2026-07-04 review found them hardcoded to '—'/'0' while the live API held data); deltas render only when real prior-period data exists (no fabricated trends)

The dashboard gains its FIRST /v1/events consumer (none exists today): a resilient SSE client (reconnect + backoff; auth-aware once F-045 lands) feeds a live Recent-activity feed (document.ingested/removed, memory.captured, scan lifecycle) replacing the permanent empty state, and the notifications bell (today an honest 'all caught up' shell) shows the same events with unread state
Loading/empty/error states; WCAG AA (axe) + screenshot-verified; e2e (an emitted event appears in the feed); workspace gates green
features.notes: The bell/feed here is LIVE-SESSION only (SSE, lost on reload). The persistent, per-user notification service (store + read state + preferences + agent-readable surface) is F-065 and builds on this.

features.title: Dashboard: search depth — excerpts, kind filters, result detail view, keyboard nav, virtualization
acceptance: Search results show content excerpts with query highlighting (extend the /v1/search response ADDITIVELY with an optional snippet; OpenAPI + SDK regenerated) — today a result is only ref + score + signals

Results stop being a dead end: selecting one opens a detail surface rendering the fragment/file/memory body with full provenance (per-signal rank/score/weight) and actions — 'open in Inspector as task seed' and 'show effects' — wiring search → compile → effects into one investigation flow
Kind filters (file/memory/symbol) + result counts; full keyboard navigation (arrows/enter, focus management); the results list is virtualized for large sets (first real FR-49 virtualization)
WCAG AA (axe) + screenshot-verified; RTL + e2e; workspace gates green

features.title: Dashboard: Inspector v2 — honest empty guidance, agent-ready export, compile filters & presets
acceptance: An empty package renders a GUIDANCE state instead of perfect scores (the 2026-07-04 live review compiled against an empty corpus and got 'Budget adherence 100% · Provenance coverage 100% · 0 fragments' — misleading): explain that retrieval matched nothing, link to Sources; package scores render only when fragmentCount > 0
Agent-ready export: one-click copy of the compiled package as citation-preserving Markdown (sections + refs + why-included) and JSON download; per-fragment copy — the human↔agent handoff path

Compile controls: CompileRequest kind filters surfaced in the form; budget presets; when the F-035 entitlement clamp reduces the requested budget the UI says so explicitly; a session-local recent-compiles list re-runs prior tasks
RTL + e2e; WCAG AA (axe) + screenshot-verified; workspace gates green

features.title: Dashboard: enterprise data-table standard + Audit v2 (real pagination, actor/date filters, export)
acceptance: A reusable data-table pattern (TanStack Table + virtualization; sortable columns, sticky header, cursor pagination, truncation with tooltips) established once and adopted by the audit view first; the memory/sources tables (F-041/F-042) build on it

Audit v2: REAL cursor pagination using the existing nextCursor (replaces the 'narrow the filters to see older entries' hint found in review), plus actor and date-range filters (GET /v1/audit already supports actor/since/until — the UI exposes only action/outcome today)
Export of the filtered audit view as CSV/JSON (compliance workflow); the export action is itself audited
Table semantics preserved under virtualization (WCAG AA, axe); RTL + e2e; workspace gates green

features.title: Dashboard: UX-baseline completion + i18n readiness (FR-49 debt, NFR-14)
acceptance: The FR-49 baseline is audited claim-by-claim and closed (the 2026-07-04 review found several claimed items unimplemented): virtualized lists on every long list, optimistic updates where safe (memory capture), context menus on data rows (copy ref / open / show effects), a keyboard-shortcuts help overlay, and the ⌘K palette covering every route + primary action
i18n readiness (NFR-14): user-facing strings externalized into a locale catalog (English only; no translation yet); a lint guard prevents new hardcoded strings in components
Theme + responsive parity: a screenshot matrix (light/dark × desktop/mobile) across all routes; reduced-motion behavior verified
axe WCAG AA re-run across every route; workspace gates green

features.title: Notification service: persistent per-user notification center + preferences + agent-readable surface
acceptance: Notification domain + NotificationStore port with in-memory (reference) + persistent SQLite adapters (shared conformance suite incl. tenant isolation): typed kinds (scan.completed/failed, memory.captured, token.created/revoked, plan.changed, system.alert) with severity, tenant + recipient scoping, per-user read/unread state, retention prune (mirrors the F-027 audit-log pattern); produced from the SAME domain events that feed SSE — never a second event taxonomy

REST + MCP surface (ADR-0036 parity): GET /v1/notifications (filter unread/kind/severity, cursor-paginated) + mark-read/mark-all-read + per-user notification preferences (which kinds notify) — Zod → OpenAPI + regenerated SDK; a list_notifications MCP tool so a RECONNECTING AGENT can ask 'what changed since my last session' (agent-first differentiator, token-lean); notification reads/preference writes audited where sensitive

Dashboard: the F-060 bell upgrades to a persistent notification center (unread badge survives reload; live via SSE, history via the API; F-063 data-table/virtualization pattern for the full list) + a preferences section (settings/profile); honest states; WCAG AA (axe) + screenshot-verified

Additive & back-compatible (default on with quiet sensible kinds; zero-auth Local maps to the default principal); outbound channels (email/Slack/webhook) are DOCUMENTED SEAMS behind a NotificationChannel port — not built until a customer needs them; unit + e2e (emit → persisted → listed → mark-read → badge clears); workspace gates green
